name: Brownie Cakes in a Jar
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease two 1-quart straight-sided wide-mouth canning jars. Line the bottom of each jar with waxed paper. Set aside.
2. In a small bowl, mix together flour, sugar, baking soda, and cinnamon (if desired). Set aside.
3. In a medium saucepan, combine margarine, water, and cocoa powder. Heat and stir until the margarine is melted and the mixture is well blended. Remove from heat and stir in the flour mixture.
4. Add buttermilk, egg, and vanilla; beat by hand until smooth. Stir in nuts.
5. Pour the mixture into the prepared canning jars. Cover the jars tightly with greased foil (grease the side that will touch the jar). Place the foil in place.
6. Place the jars in a 3-1/2 to 6-quart crockery cooker with liner in place. Cover; cook on high heat setting for 2 3/4 to 3 hours, or until cakes spring back when touched and a long wooden toothpick inserted near the centers comes out clean.
7. Remove jars from cooker; let cool for 10 minutes. Unmold cakes; remove waxed paper. Serve warm or cool with ice cream.
